journal_entry.description: Payment of accounts payable by cheque
journal_entry.rationale: Settling accounts payable reduces the liability (debit Accounts Payable) and paying by cheque reduces the cash asset (credit Cash). The tuples correctly indicate one liability decrease (debit) and one asset decrease (credit). No tax applies as this is a payment of an existing liability, not a new purchase.

output.reason: Settling accounts payable means paying off a liability. Debiting a liability decreases its balance. This is 1 liability decrease line.

debit_classifier.reason: Settling accounts payable means paying off a liability. Debiting a liability decreases its balance. This is 1 liability decrease line.

output.reason: Issuing a cheque means cash is leaving the business. Cash is an asset, and when cash leaves, the asset decreases. This is recorded as a credit to the Cash/Bank account (asset decrease).

credit_classifier.reason: Issuing a cheque means cash is leaving the business. Cash is an asset, and when cash leaves, the asset decreases. This is recorded as a credit to the Cash/Bank account (asset decrease).
